response:
<<Order by Length, Then by Name>>

```sql
SELECT title, director
FROM movies
ORDER BY 
    LENGTH(title) DESC,
    title COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci,
    LENGTH(director) DESC,
    director COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
```

This SQL query organizes records by the length of the `title` column in descending order. If two titles have the same length, it sorts them alphabetically. It then does the same for the `director` column. The use of `COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci` ensures case-insensitive and accent-insensitive sorting, which is commonly desired for names.

A palindrome: **"madam"**.
